# Vocabulary Tools for Agentic Systems


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

# Building Vocabulary Tools

In this notebook, we’ll create specialized tools for working with
semantic vocabularies, extending the agent infrastructure from the main
CLI module. These tools will allow agents to:

1.  **Explore and manage vocabulary registries** - Browse, search, and
    manipulate entries in the registry
2.  **Compose and analyze contexts** - Create, merge, and analyze
    JSON-LD contexts
3.  **Retrieve and process vocabulary resources** - Fetch, convert, and
    validate vocabulary data
4.  **Integrate with Wikidata** - Search, align, and enrich data using
    Wikidata

We’ll organize these tools into logical groups and create a specialized
agent class that comes pre-configured with all the vocabulary tools.

# 1. Registry Tools

First, let’s create tools for exploring and managing the vocabulary
registry. These tools will wrap the functionality in
`vocab/registry.py`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/la3d/cogitarelink/blob/main/cogitarelink/cli/vocab_tools.py#L23"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### registry_tools

>  registry_tools (registry=None)

\*Create a set of tools for working with the vocabulary registry.

Returns: Dict mapping tool names to tool functions\*

# Let’s test our registry tools

``` python
reg_tools = registry_tools()

# Explore available vocabularies
reg_tools["explore_registry"]()
```

# 2. Context Tools

Next, let’s create tools for composing and analyzing JSON-LD contexts.
These tools will wrap the functionality in `vocab/composer.py` and
`vocab/collision.py`.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/la3d/cogitarelink/blob/main/cogitarelink/cli/vocab_tools.py#L231"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### context_tools

>  context_tools (composer=None, resolver=None)

\*Create a set of tools for working with JSON-LD contexts.

Returns: Dict mapping tool names to tool functions\*

# Let’s test our context tools

``` python
ctx_tools = context_tools()

# Compose a context with multiple vocabularies
ctx_tools["compose_context"](prefixes=["schema", "dc"])
```

    {'success': False,
     'error': "Client error '404 Not Found' for url 'https://www.dublincore.org/contexts/dc-terms/'\nFor more information check: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/404"}

``` python
# Analyze compatibility between two vocabularies
ctx_tools["analyze_context_compatibility"]("schema", "dc")
```

    {'success': False,
     'error': "Failed to analyze compatibility: Client error '404 Not Found' for url 'https://www.dublincore.org/contexts/dc-terms/'\nFor more information check: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/404"}

# 3. Retrieval Tools

Now, let’s create tools for retrieving and processing vocabulary
resources. These tools will wrap the functionality in
`integration/retriever.py`.

### retrieval_tools

\*Create a set of tools for retrieving and processing vocabulary
resources.

Returns: Dict mapping tool names to tool functions\*

# Let’s test our retrieval tools with a Wikidata search

``` python
ret_tools = retrieval_tools()

# Search Wikidata for a term
ret_tools["wikidata_search"]("machine learning", limit=3)
```

``` python
# Let's test our multi-format metadata extraction
html_example = """
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
    <title>Product Page Example</title>
    <!-- OpenGraph metadata -->
    <meta property="og:title" content="Sample Product" />
    <meta property="og:type" content="product" />
    <meta property="og:url" content="https://example.com/product/123" />
    <meta property="og:image" content="https://example.com/product/123.jpg" />
    
    <!-- JSON-LD metadata -->
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
        "@context": "https://schema.org/",
        "@type": "Product",
        "name": "Sample Product",
        "description": "This is a sample product for testing",
        "sku": "SAMPLE123",
        "brand": {
            "@type": "Brand",
            "name": "Test Brand"
        },
        "offers": {
            "@type": "Offer",
            "price": 19.99,
            "priceCurrency": "USD",
            "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock"
        }
    }
    </script>
    
    <!-- RDFa metadata -->
    <div vocab="https://schema.org/" typeof="Organization">
        <span property="name">Example Company</span>
        <div property="address" typeof="PostalAddress">
            <span property="streetAddress">123 Main St</span>
            <span property="addressLocality">Anytown</span>
        </div>
    </div>
</head>
<body>
    <h1>Sample Product</h1>
    <p>This is a sample product for testing metadata extraction.</p>
</body>
</html>
"""

# Extract all formats
multi_result = ret_tools["extract_metadata"](html_content=html_example, base_url="https://example.com/")
print(f"Found {multi_result.get('total_items', 0)} metadata items in {multi_result.get('formats', [])} formats")
multi_result.get('counts', {})
```

    Found 2 metadata items in ['json-ld', 'opengraph'] formats

    {'json-ld': 1, 'opengraph': 1}

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/la3d/cogitarelink/blob/main/cogitarelink/cli/vocab_tools.py#L739"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### VocabToolAgent

>  VocabToolAgent (name:str='vocab-agent')

*Agent with specialized tools for working with semantic vocabularies.*

# Let’s test our VocabToolAgent

``` python
vocab_agent = VocabToolAgent(name="test-vocab-agent")

# List all available tools
vocab_agent.run_tool("list_tools")
```

    [{'name': 'get_version',
      'description': 'Get the CogitareLink version',
      'signature': {}},
     {'name': 'list_tools',
      'description': 'List all available tools',
      'signature': {}},
     {'name': 'get_agent_memory',
      'description': 'Get all items in agent memory',
      'signature': {}},
     {'name': 'explore_registry',
      'description': '\n        Explore available vocabularies in the registry with optional filtering.\n\n        Parameters:\n            filter_tags: Optional tags to filter vocabularies\n\n        Returns:\n            Dictionary of vocabulary entries matching the filter\n        ',
      'signature': {'filter_tags': 'List[str]'}},
     {'name': 'get_vocabulary_info',
      'description': '\n        Get detailed information about a specific vocabulary.\n\n        Parameters:\n            prefix: Vocabulary prefix\n\n        Returns:\n            Complete vocabulary entry information\n        ',
      'signature': {'prefix': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'add_temp_vocabulary',
      'description': '\n        Add a temporary vocabulary to the registry for the current session.\n\n        Parameters:\n            prefix: Short prefix for the vocabulary\n            uri: Primary URI for the vocabulary\n            context_url: Optional JSON-LD context URL\n            inline_context: Optional inline context definition\n            ttl_url: Optional TTL file URL to derive context from\n\n        Returns:\n            Dictionary with operation result\n        ',
      'signature': {'prefix': 'str',
       'uri': 'str',
       'context_url': 'str',
       'inline_context': 'Dict',
       'ttl_url': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'detect_vocabularies',
      'description': '\n        Detect which vocabularies are used in a given context.\n\n        Parameters:\n            context_list: JSON-LD context to analyze (string, dict, or list)\n\n        Returns:\n            List of detected vocabulary prefixes with confidence scores\n        ',
      'signature': {'context_list': 'Union[List, Dict, str]'}},
     {'name': 'compose_context',
      'description': '\n        Compose a JSON-LD context from multiple vocabulary prefixes.\n\n        Parameters:\n            prefixes: List of vocabulary prefixes, ordered by priority\n            support_nest: Whether to add @nest support\n            propagate: Whether to allow context propagation\n\n        Returns:\n            Composed context object ready to use in a JSON-LD document\n        ',
      'signature': {'prefixes': 'List[str]',
       'support_nest': 'bool',
       'propagate': 'bool'}},
     {'name': 'analyze_context_compatibility',
      'description': '\n        Analyze compatibility between two vocabularies.\n\n        Parameters:\n            prefix_a: First vocabulary prefix\n            prefix_b: Second vocabulary prefix\n\n        Returns:\n            Compatibility analysis with recommended strategy\n        ',
      'signature': {'prefix_a': 'str', 'prefix_b': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'load_context_for_vocabulary',
      'description': '\n        Load the context for a specific vocabulary.\n\n        Parameters:\n            prefix: Vocabulary prefix\n\n        Returns:\n            JSON-LD context for the vocabulary\n        ',
      'signature': {'prefix': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'apply_collision_strategy',
      'description': '\n        Apply a collision strategy to a document with multiple vocabularies.\n\n        Parameters:\n            data: JSON-LD document to process\n            prefixes: List of vocabulary prefixes to consider\n\n        Returns:\n            Processed document with collision strategy applied\n        ',
      'signature': {'data': 'Dict[str, Any]', 'prefixes': 'List[str]'}},
     {'name': 'retrieve_vocabulary_resource',
      'description': '\n        Retrieve a vocabulary resource with automatic format detection.\n\n        Parameters:\n            uri: URI of the resource to retrieve\n            format_hint: Optional hint about the expected format\n\n        Returns:\n            Retrieved resource converted to JSON-LD\n        ',
      'signature': {'uri': 'str', 'format_hint': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'extract_metadata',
      'description': "\n        Extract metadata from HTML in multiple formats using extruct.\n\n        Parameters:\n            html_content: HTML content to analyze\n            base_url: Base URL for resolving relative URIs\n            formats: List of formats to extract ('json-ld','rdfa','microdata','opengraph','microformat')\n                    Default: all formats\n\n        Returns:\n            Dictionary with metadata for each format\n        ",
      'signature': {'html_content': 'str',
       'base_url': 'str',
       'formats': 'List[str]'}},
     {'name': 'extract_embedded_jsonld',
      'description': '\n        Extract JSON-LD content embedded in HTML.\n\n        Parameters:\n            html_content: HTML content to analyze\n            base_url: Base URL for resolving relative URIs\n\n        Returns:\n            Extracted JSON-LD data if found\n        ',
      'signature': {'html_content': 'str', 'base_url': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'convert_format',
      'description': '\n        Convert between different RDF serialization formats.\n\n        Parameters:\n            content: Content to convert\n            from_format: Source format (turtle, xml, n3, etc.)\n            to_format: Target format (defaults to json-ld)\n\n        Returns:\n            Converted content\n        ',
      'signature': {'content': 'str', 'from_format': 'str', 'to_format': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'wikidata_search',
      'description': '\n        Search for entities in Wikidata matching a query.\n\n        Parameters:\n            query: Search text\n            limit: Maximum number of results\n            language: Language code for labels\n\n        Returns:\n            List of matching Wikidata entities\n        ',
      'signature': {'query': 'str', 'limit': 'int', 'language': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'wikidata_entity_details',
      'description': '\n        Get detailed information about a Wikidata entity.\n\n        Parameters:\n            entity_id: Wikidata entity ID (Q-number)\n\n        Returns:\n            Detailed entity information\n        ',
      'signature': {'entity_id': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'align_property_to_wikidata',
      'description': '\n        Find the closest matching Wikidata property for a dataset field.\n\n        Parameters:\n            property_name: Name of the property to align\n            description: Optional property description\n\n        Returns:\n            Wikidata alignment suggestions\n        ',
      'signature': {'property_name': 'str', 'description': 'str'}},
     {'name': 'create_croissant_dataset',
      'description': '\n            Create a Croissant dataset with the specified properties.\n\n            Parameters:\n                name: Dataset name\n                description: Optional dataset description\n                recordsets: Optional list of recordset definitions\n\n            Returns:\n                Complete Croissant dataset object\n            ',
      'signature': {'name': 'str',
       'description': 'str',
       'recordsets': 'List[Dict]'}},
     {'name': 'align_dataset_to_wikidata',
      'description': '\n            Align a Croissant dataset fields with Wikidata concepts.\n\n            Parameters:\n                dataset: Croissant dataset to align\n                confidence_threshold: Minimum confidence for automatic alignment\n\n            Returns:\n                Dataset with Wikidata alignments added\n            ',
      'signature': {'dataset': 'Dict[str, Any]', 'confidence_threshold': 'float'}}]

``` python
# Create a Croissant dataset
croissant_dataset = vocab_agent.run_tool("create_croissant_dataset", 
                                        name="Weather Measurements 2023",
                                        description="Daily temperature and precipitation measurements for 2023",
                                        recordsets=[{
                                            "@type": "RecordSet",
                                            "name": "weather_data",
                                            "field": [
                                                {"@type": "Property", "name": "date", "dataType": "date", "description": "Measurement date"},
                                                {"@type": "Property", "name": "temperature", "dataType": "float", "description": "Temperature in Celsius"},
                                                {"@type": "Property", "name": "precipitation", "dataType": "float", "description": "Rainfall in millimeters"}
                                            ]
                                        }])
```

``` python
croissant_dataset
```

    {'success': True,
     'dataset': {'@context': {'@vocab': 'http://mlcommons.org/croissant/'},
      '@type': 'Dataset',
      'name': 'Weather Measurements 2023',
      'description': 'Daily temperature and precipitation measurements for 2023',
      'recordSet': [{'@type': 'RecordSet',
        'name': 'weather_data',
        'field': [{'@type': 'Property',
          'name': 'date',
          'dataType': 'date',
          'description': 'Measurement date'},
         {'@type': 'Property',
          'name': 'temperature',
          'dataType': 'float',
          'description': 'Temperature in Celsius'},
         {'@type': 'Property',
          'name': 'precipitation',
          'dataType': 'float',
          'description': 'Rainfall in millimeters'}]}]}}

``` python
# Align dataset with Wikidata
aligned_dataset = vocab_agent.run_tool("align_dataset_to_wikidata", 
                                     dataset=croissant_dataset.get("dataset", {}),
                                     confidence_threshold=0.6)
```

``` python
# Show alignments
aligned_dataset.get("alignments", {})
```

    {}

# Conclusion

In this notebook, we’ve built a set of vocabulary tools that extend our
agent infrastructure:

1.  **Registry Tools**: For exploring, querying, and manipulating the
    vocabulary registry
2.  **Context Tools**: For composing, analyzing, and working with
    JSON-LD contexts
3.  **Retrieval Tools**: For fetching, converting, and processing
    semantic data
4.  **Croissant Tools**: Specialized tools for working with Croissant
    datasets

These tools are bundled into a
[`VocabToolAgent`](https://la3d.github.io/cogitarelink/vocab_tools.html#vocabtoolagent)
class that makes them easily accessible and handles error cases
gracefully. The agent includes robust fallback mechanisms for vocabulary
resolution, ensuring it can work even when primary sources are
unavailable.

Our implementation leverages CogitareLink’s existing systems for
vocabulary management while providing a clean, tool-based interface that
follows the design principles of our agent infrastructure.

In the next notebook, we can explore how to use this agent for more
complex data workflows, such as:

1.  Multi-vocabulary dataset creation
2.  Schema alignment with external ontologies
3.  Automated metadata extraction and enrichment
4.  Integration with Jupyter notebooks for interactive data exploration
